question: A process definition version has 3 running instances. An operator filters the **Processes** page by that name and version and clicks **Delete** at the top right. According to the docs, what is the result?

options.text: Deletion is not possible until the running instances are cancelled or resolved.
The running instances are migrated automatically to the next available version.
The running instances continue independently of the deleted definition.
The running instances are soft-cancelled with a 7-day grace period before final removal.

a.text: The docs explicitly note that the selected process definition version must have no running instances, otherwise it is not possible to delete the definition; running instances must first be cancelled or resolved.

b.text: Deleting a process definition does not trigger automatic migration of running instances to another version.

c.text: Running instances cannot continue without their process definition; the delete is blocked while instances are still running.

d.text: There is no documented 7-day soft-cancel grace period; the delete is simply blocked while running instances exist.

explanation: Operate refuses to delete a process definition version that has running instances. The docs direct the operator to first cancel or resolve running instances from the process instances list or instance detail page, then retry the deletion.
